articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Re-industrialising Zimbabwe: From Extraction to Production in a Fragmented Global Economy – Comparative Lessons and Strategic Imperatives
more.description: Zimbabwe’s economic predicament is often attributed to currency instability, sanctions, or governance failures. While these factors are real and influential, they obscure a deeper structural issue: Zimbabwe is not impoverished because it lacks resources, talent, or geography. By Brighton Musonza Its challenge is fundamentally structural—an economy that has failed to industrialise. Without a productive industrial […]The post Re-industrialising Zimbabwe: From Extraction to Production in a Fragmented Global Economy – Comparative Lessons and Strategic Imperatives appeared first on The...

more.title: Econet InfraCo and Cassava Technologies: Engineering Africa’s Distributed Digital Infrastructure Economy
more.description: The emergence and listing of Econet InfraCo represents a decisive structural shift in how African telecommunications groups are reconfiguring themselves for the data economy. No longer confined to voice and connectivity, the sector is undergoing a deep transformation into a capital-intensive, compute-driven infrastructure class. When viewed in tandem with Cassava Technologies and its backbone network […]The post Econet InfraCo and Cassava Technologies: Engineering Africa’s Distributed Digital Infrastructure Economy appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: First Mutual Returns to Profit, but Property Gains Mask Underwriting Pressures
more.description: HARARE – First Mutual Holdings has reported a return to profitability for the year ended 31 December 2025, posting a net profit of US$14.3 million after a US$26.3 million loss in the prior year. However, analysts caution that the recovery was largely driven by property revaluation gains rather than core insurance performance. According to Equity […]The post First Mutual Returns to Profit, but Property Gains Mask Underwriting Pressures appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Iran Seizes Two Tankers in Strait of Hormuz, Raising Tensions in Key Global Shipping Route
more.description: TEHRAN/DUBAI – Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has seized two commercial vessels in the strategically important Strait of Hormuz, increasing tensions in one of the world’s most critical oil transit corridors. According to Iranian state-linked media, citing an official statement from the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy, the vessels MSC Francesca and Epaminondas were […]The post Iran Seizes Two Tankers in Strait of Hormuz, Raising Tensions in Key Global Shipping Route appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Telecel Zimbabwe Opens Investor Search as Rescue Plan Gains Momentum
more.description: HARARE – Telecel Zimbabwe entered corporate rescue after a prolonged period of financial strain marked by mounting debt obligations, declining subscriber numbers, and intensifying competition in Zimbabwe’s telecoms sector. The company has struggled to keep pace with larger rivals, particularly in network investment and service quality, which has eroded its market share over time. The […]The post Telecel Zimbabwe Opens Investor Search as Rescue Plan Gains Momentum appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Ariston Bets on New CEO as Turnaround Nears Inflection Point Amid Climate and Funding Pressure
more.description: HARARE – Ariston Holdings Limited has appointed Farai Madziva as its new Chief Executive Officer, marking a leadership transition the board says will steer the agro-industrial group through the final stage of its turnaround and capital-raising programme. According to Equity Axis News, Madziva officially assumed the role following the departure of outgoing CEO Leon Nortier […]The post Ariston Bets on New CEO as Turnaround Nears Inflection Point Amid Climate and Funding Pressure appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Highlanders in Deep Financial Crisis As Players Boycott Training, Mwaruwari Owed Salaries
more.description: [New Zimbabwe] Highlanders Football Club are facing a deepening financial crisis, with players reportedly going unpaid since February.

more.title: Government Pleads With Striking Nurses to Return to Work
more.description: [New Zimbabwe] Discover moreZimbabwean art printsCyber security solutionsZimbabwean fashion apparelZimbabwe's government scrambled into crisis mode on Monday as a nationwide nurses' strike crippled public hospitals, forcing Health Minister Douglas Mombeshora to make an urgent appeal for the healthworkers to return to work while promising to address their grievances.

more.title: PSL to Probe Hardrock FC Marshal's Altercation With Supporter
more.description: [New Zimbabwe] The Zimbabwe Premier Soccer League (PSL) has launched an investigation into a violent incident involving a Hardrock FC marshal and a supporter during a league match played over the weekend.

more.title: Man Axes Stepmother, Sets Her On Fire
more.description: [New Zimbabwe] Discover moreWorld news updatesDigital marketing servicesZimbabwean sports merchandiseA 20-year-old Lupane man who struck his elderly stepmother with an axe before setting her on fire in a dispute over cooking oil has been jailed for 16 years.

more.title: African Sun exits VFEX, opts for unlisted status
more.description: HARARE,– Hospitality group African Sun Limited has delisted from the Victoria Falls Stock Exchange (VFEX), signalling a strategic shift to operate as an unlisted public company. In a notice to shareholders, the group confirmed it had met all regulatory conditions for the voluntary termination of its listing, with trading in its shares ceasing on April […]The post African Sun exits VFEX, opts for unlisted status appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra review – A productivity and privacy-first flagship smartphone
more.description: Modern smartphones have become mobile workstations – and after spending a few days with the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra, it’s clear this device is designed with that shift in mind. This is a flagship that prioritises productivity, security, and efficiency, while still delivering top-tier performance and creative capability when needed. more.title: High Court finds miners in contempt over Lonrho site
more.description: The High Court of Zimbabwe has declared a mining company and its officials in contempt of court for defying an interdict barring operations at the disputed Lonrho Mining site. In Asmdev Incorporated (Pvt) Ltd v Joh and Lewis Investment (Pvt) Ltd and Others (544/26) [2026] ZWHHC 130, Justice Mambara ruled that the respondents wilfully breached […]The post High Court finds miners in contempt over Lonrho site appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: State not ready to prosecute Biti
more.description: The trial of former Finance Minister and Constitution Defenders Forum (CDF) Convener Tendai Biti, who is facing charges of holding a public meeting without notifying the regulating authority, has been postponed to May 26, 2026. The matter was deferred on Monday by Mutare Magistrate Honest Musiiwa after the State indicated it was not ready to […]The post State not ready to prosecute Biti appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: U.S. Commits to Deepen Trade Ties With Zimbabwe
more.description: [New Zimbabwe] Discover moreBusiness directory listingsLocal food productsHealth community newsTHE United States Embassy in Zimbabwe has reaffirmed its commitment to deepen trade ties with Zimbabwe by scaling up relevant exchange programs between the two nations.
